page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 virtual CPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel processors. The dedicated x86_64 architecture operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz with 2.0 threads per core. This configuration is supported by the Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated resource allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, it includes 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This combination of high-capacity RAM and direct-attached NVMe storage supports workloads requiring rapid data access and high-throughput local scratch space.

Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps, and the instance is optimized for both network and Elastic Block Store performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to the CPU allocation positions this instance for resource-intensive workloads that would otherwise require larger, more expensive cluster configurations.

This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy applications. Suitable workloads include large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, real-time data analytics, and enterprise-grade enterprise resource planning systems that benefit from high network throughput and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.9 GHz on Intel processors. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, alongside 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps, with optimizations for both network and EBS throughput. This high-density memory and storage profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for consolidating large databases and in-memory applications. It is suited for enterprise databases, real-time data processing, and memory-heavy analytical workloads.
